origin.name: Uganda
origin.key_document: Death certificate from local government (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Uganda travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Kampala guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Uganda is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from local government (English language)
special_rules: Uganda issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Ugandan authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Ugandan documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Kampala can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Uganda to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Uganda. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Local registration, police clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
